steps.title: Understand the structure
steps.content:
The passage has two parts: (1) decades of failed theories about chondrule origins, and (2) scientists finally finding strong evidence. The blank needs a transition that signals the long search came to an end in success — not another example, result, or similarity.
  • Choice A — 'For example,': signals an illustration of what came before — but finding evidence isn't an example of the failed theories ✗
  • Choice B — 'Therefore,': signals the evidence is a logical result of the failed theories — but the evidence resolves the debate, it doesn't follow from failure ✗
  • Choice C — 'Similarly,': signals a parallel to the failed theories — but finding strong evidence is the opposite of those failures ✗
  • Choice D — 'Finally,': signals the conclusion of a prolonged effort, fitting perfectly after "decades" of failure ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content:
The word "decades" establishes a long search, and "none had sufficient support" confirms each theory failed. "Finally" is the only word that marks the moment a prolonged effort ends in success rather than framing the finding as another example, consequence, or parallel.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
